text: This is a case of a sixty-three-year-old male who admitted to the ER with the complaint of left lower quadrant pain. He had a history of three episodes of diverticulitis and no history of surgery.

Abdominal CT revealed pelvic abscess and suspected perforation due to diverticulitis. This CT scan shows the diverticulitis of descending and sigmoid columns and also pelvic abscess secondary to the perforation.

The surgeon performs an air leak test on the anastomosis site to visually assess its integrity, ensuring there are no leaks in the newly formed connection between the colon segments.
